title: N-able Inc. (NABL) Expands Technology Alliance and AI SoC Capabilities
content:
N-able, Inc. (NYSE:NABL) is one of the best cheap penny stocks to buy that aren’t scams. On April 2, N-able, Inc. (NYSE:NABL) expanded its Technology Alliance Program with the addition of Zensec and Atomatik.N-able Inc. (NABL) Expands Technology Alliance and AI SoC Capabilities

Copyright: melpomen / 123RF Stock Photo

The addition affirms the company’s commitment to delivering exceptional value through trusted partnerships. In addition, the integration of the two companies’ technologies will enable customers to build resilient, secure, and efficient technology environments.

The alliance is a strategic initiative that seeks to bring together industry-leading technology companies to ensure secure integration across N-able’s business resilience platform. Zensec joins the alliance with structure expert-led incident response services, as Atomatik adds automation capabilities for streamlining repeatable rules-driven security and IT workflows.

Earlier, N-able expanded its AI-driven Security Operations Center (SOC) capabilities through Adlumin Managed Detection. By enhancing its capabilities, the company is helping organizations identify and stop stealthy cyberattacks. The AI-driven approach helps IT teams detect and respond to cyberattacks earlier and uncover subtle, high-risk activity.

N-able Inc. (NYSE:NABL) provides cloud-first software solutions—including remote monitoring and management (RMM), data protection, and AI-powered security—designed for managed service providers (MSPs) to secure and manage IT infrastructure for small and mid-market businesses. They enable IT professionals to monitor, manage, and protect devices and networks from a unified dashboard, focusing on cyber resilience.

While we acknowledge the potential of NABL as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. title: How The Narrative On N-able (NABL) Is Shifting As Growth And Valuation Tension Builds
content:
Find winning stocks in any market cycle. Join 7 million investors using Simply Wall St's investing ideas for FREE.

N-able’s latest analyst update keeps the model fair value anchored at $7.35, signaling no change to the central price target. The lack of movement here lines up with research that balances optimism around the business model with caution on execution, growth visibility, and what analysts are willing to pay for the shares today. As you read on, you will see how this steady fair value fits into the broader, evolving narrative around N-able and what to watch next.

Analyst Price Targets don't always capture the full story. Head over to our Company Report to find new ways to value N-able.

What Wall Street Has Been Saying

🐂 Bullish Takeaways

B. Riley has initiated coverage with a bullish view, signaling that some research houses see upside potential in N-able’s current setup and business model. Scotiabank highlights that N-able is, in its view, well insulated from copycat risk, which supports the idea that the product set and positioning may be defensible against competition.

🐻 Bearish Takeaways

Scotiabank cut its price target on N-able from US$8.75 to US$5.25 and kept a Sector Perform rating. This points to a more cautious stance on how much investors might reasonably pay for the shares right now. Scotiabank also notes that it has not yet seen AI acting as a clear tailwind for the business. The firm points to the need for top line acceleration in 2026 before expecting a potential re rating, putting execution and growth delivery in clear focus for investors.

Do your thoughts align with the Bull or Bear Analysts? Perhaps you think there's more to the story. Head to the Simply Wall St Community to discover more perspectives!NYSE:NABL 1-Year Stock Price Chart

See how N-able's fair value stacks up across multiple valuation models — not just analyst targets.

What's in the News

N-able issued guidance for Q1 2026 revenue of US$131 million to US$132 million and full year 2026 revenue of US$554 million to US$559 million. The company completed a share repurchase of 3,776,155 shares, or 2.01% of shares outstanding, for US$29.93 million under a buyback program announced on March 12, 2025. N-able expanded AI capabilities across its platform for unified endpoint management, security operations, and data protection, with an emphasis on automation, threat detection, and AI-assisted recovery. The company added Zensec and Atomatik to its Technology Alliance Program and was selected as Manchester City’s official cybersecurity partner across the club’s digital and physical assets.

Story Continues

How This Changes the Fair Value For N-able

Fair value remains at $7.35 as the central valuation estimate. The revenue growth assumption is effectively stable at 8.50%, with only a very small numerical refinement. The net profit margin assumption is effectively stable at 10.37%, with only a minor rounding difference. The future P/E multiple is adjusted slightly from 26.77x to 26.68x. The discount rate is adjusted slightly from 9.87% to 9.75%.

Never Miss an Update: Follow The Narrative

Narratives connect N-able’s business story to the assumptions behind its earnings forecasts and fair value, so you can see how the numbers tie back to real world drivers. They update over time as new data, guidance, and risks are incorporated.

Head over to the Simply Wall St Community and follow the Narrative on N-able to stay up to date on:

How growing demand for integrated cyber resilience platforms and cloud adoption shapes N-able’s recurring revenue opportunity. What expanding reseller and IT channel partnerships, cross selling and international expansion could mean for customer reach and profitability. Key risks from industry consolidation, potential commoditization of core security tools and the execution challenges around AI features and mid market expansion.

title: A Subtle Upgrade at N-able (NABL) That Could Shift the Cybersecurity Game
content:
N-able, Inc. (NYSE:NABL) is among the 12 Cheap Penny Stocks to Invest In Now.

On March 25, N-able, Inc. (NYSE:NABL) introduced new AI-driven detection capabilities within its Security Operations Center offering through Adlumin Managed Detection and Response. These enhancements include advanced threat detection models such as anomalous PowerShell activity, DNS disruptions, and irregular process execution, enabling organizations to identify and mitigate increasingly sophisticated cyber threats. This expansion strengthens N-able’s value proposition in cybersecurity and enhances its ability to deliver proactive, AI-powered protection for enterprise clients.

On March 23, William Blair downgraded N-able, Inc. (NYSE:NABL) to Underperform from Outperform, citing heightened uncertainty across the infrastructure software sector driven by the rapid emergence of AI. The firm noted that companies in this space are entering a transitional phase requiring adjustments to product strategy, pricing, and go-to-market approaches. While this introduces near-term uncertainty, it also underscores the importance of innovation, which N-able is actively addressing through its AI-driven enhancements.

N-able, Inc. (NYSE:NABL) provides cloud-based software solutions for managed service providers, focusing on IT management and cybersecurity. Founded in 2000 and headquartered in Ottawa, Canada, the company’s continued investment in AI-powered security tools positions it to capitalize on growing demand for advanced threat detection and IT infrastructure resilience.

While we acknowledge the potential of NABL as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk.
